message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My task: add opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, emit, flush, reset). Only touches dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: My task: add idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat to StreamListener in streaming_listener.py. I saw mention of an is_last_chunk field being a separate feature - if that's your task, let me know which files it touches (messages.py for the StreamResponse dataclass? other streaming_listener.py call sites?) so we don't collide. I'll leave a clear integration point (e.g. setattr only if the field exists) for is_last_chunk on my new timeout-induced StreamResponse.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both genuinely need this single file (no other file fits either feature), so let's keep edits additive/non-overlapping in scope: I will only touch __init__ (add idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts), add one line at the top of receive() to refresh self._last_chunk_ts when a non-empty chunk arrives, and append a new tick(now_ts=None) method after flush(). I won't touch your logging call sites inside the existing branches of receive()/flush(). Go ahead with your debug/trace logging additions - as long as you don't remove/rename receive()/flush()/__init__ signatures wholesale we should merge cleanly. Starting implementation now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Both our features land in streaming_listener.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible here - let's split by region instead, additive-only, to avoid clobbering. My planned touches: (1) __init__ signature: append 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' as NEW trailing params (won't touch allow_reuse or earlier params), plus 'import logging' at top and self._logger init + docstring lines at the end of __init__ body. (2) In receive(): guarded debug.isEnabledFor log lines added right after start-detection succeeds (~line 132-136 area, after self.stream_start=True), right after the rolling end-check regex match succeeds (~line 163 after self.stream_end=True), and right before the final 'return StreamResponse(...)' (~line 169) for emit-chunk logging. (3) In the allow_reuse reset block (~lines 87-93): one guarded log line after clearing state. (4) In flush(): one guarded log line near the end before return statements - I'll add it right after computing last_tokens/boundary_index, as a single trace call wrapping all return paths (may need to refactor flush() to compute result once then log then return, still same behavior). (5) A small private helper  for buffer/token truncation. I will NOT touch idle_timeout_s/tick()/heartbeat logic or add any new queue/state fields. Please avoid adding lines inside the exact same statements I listed above (e.g. don't insert your tick logic between stream_start=True and my log line) - if you need to touch those exact spots let's re-sync first. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT if so.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. To be precise on my side so you can avoid it: in __init__ I'll append idle_timeout_s: float | None = None as a NEW trailing param (after your debug/debug_logger params if you land first, or I'll append after mine - order doesn't matter, we're both just appending kwargs) plus self._last_chunk_ts = None at the end of __init__ body. In receive(), the ONLY line I add is near the top, right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message and confirms it's non-None (~line 100-103), e.g. 'self._last_chunk_ts = now_ts_or_time.time()' - I won't touch your log lines at 132-136, 163, 169, or the allow_reuse reset block. I add a brand new tick(now_ts=None) method appended after flush(). Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. I will only add: (1) trailing debug/debug_logger params to __init__ signature plus self._logger init, (2) guarded debug log calls at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk-return, allow_reuse reset block, and inside flush(); (3) a small private truncation helper. Will not touch idle_timeout_s/tick()/_last_chunk_ts or remove/rename existing methods/signatures. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts to __init__, one line in receive() to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunks, and a new tick(now_ts=None) method appended after flush() that emits an empty-chunk StreamResponse and unconditionally resets state on idle timeout. Ran tests/streaming/ - all pass. Writing patch.txt now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added debug/debug_logger params, guarded DEBUG-level trace logs at start detection (both branches), rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, and allow_reuse reset, plus a small _truncate_preview helper and _DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH=80 constant. Verified exact message formats manually with a caplog-style handler (buffer_preview correctly shows raw '\n' for the ChatAdapter example per your reasoning that lstrip would strip it otherwise). tests/streaming/ all pass (15 passed, 3 skipped), ruff clean. Writing patch.txt now.
